_doc: Layout-variant registry (composition-quality program P3). Each block declares 4-5 arrangement VARIANTS. A variant changes PLACEMENT / emphasis / structure (centered vs left-rail vs grid); the theme tokens still paint IDENTITY, so every variant stays theme-true and is built ONCE (universal, like Tier 1-3). Selection is HYBRID: data.variant overrides; else auto-pick by content shape (the `auto` map keyed by item/line count, else `default`); a no-repeat variety rule rotates among content-COMPATIBLE variants (fits=[min,max]) so a video doesn't render the same arrangement twice in a row. The block's executor branches on the resolved variant (stamped as sc['_variant'] by compose_frame._resolve_variant). Honesty-tested by tests/test_layout_variants.py: every registry block is a real BLOCK, every variant id is CONSUMED by its block (no phantom), every auto/default id is a real variant, and _resolve_variant's hybrid contract holds. NO theme weighting (variants are content-driven).

stack-left.desc: left-anchored horizontal row of 1-3 figures (the editorial classic)

centered-row.desc: the figures centered as a balanced row on the canvas

hero-single.desc: ONE oversized figure centered — a single killer number

lead-rail.desc: a big figure on an accent left rail + supporting label/context on the right

stacked-list.desc: figures stacked vertically as a left-aligned list (a set of metrics)

centered.desc: the phrase centered in the middle of the canvas

framed-card.desc: the phrase inside a bordered card, centered

banner-top.desc: top-anchored, a headline banner reading down the page

rail-accent.desc: an accent rail bar to the left of the phrase

stack.desc: title above a single vertical list (the classic)

centered.desc: centered title above a centered list

two-col.desc: title above a two-column list (for 4-6 items)

numbered-rail.desc: an accent rail with the title, the numbered list beside it

card-row.desc: each item as a small bordered card in a row/grid

centered.desc: the quote centred with the big quote-mark above (the classic)

editorial.desc: the quote left-aligned in an editorial column

side-mark.desc: an oversized quote-mark on the left, the quote beside it

framed.desc: the quote inside a bordered card

two-col.desc: the rows split across two columns (for long lists)

centered.desc: a centred kicker above a narrower centred row-list

boxed.desc: each row on its own subtle surface panel

matrix.desc: the full-width header x row-label x cell matrix (the classic)

centered.desc: a centred kicker above a narrower centred matrix

panel.desc: the matrix inside a bordered surface panel

compact.desc: tighter rows for a denser matrix

horizontal.desc: the spine runs left->right, callouts alternate up/down (the Vox default)

vertical.desc: the spine runs top->bottom, branches alternate left/right

vertical-up.desc: a vertical spine travelled bottom->top (a rising history)

split.desc: two panels side-by-side, seamless with a thin divider (the A-vs-B default)

cards.desc: two bordered cards side-by-side with a gap over the theme ground

over-under.desc: the two panels stacked top/bottom, seamless
